# llms.txt — Cyber-Arcade

> A static, gamified cyber-security learning website with no backend.

## Summary
- **Name:** Cyber-Arcade
- **Type:** Static multi-page website
- **Stack:** Modern HTML, CSS, and vanilla JavaScript (classic scripts, no build)
- **Storage:** `localStorage` on HTTP/HTTPS, `window.name` on `file://`
- **Theme:** Retro 8-bit arcade cabinet with high-contrast, accessible neon colors.
- **Styling:** Mobile-first responsive layout using reusable external CSS classes and design-token custom properties; no inline styles.
- **Deployment:** GitHub Pages, Netlify, Vercel, or any static CDN
- **Repo root:** `C:\Projects\Devin-Agent-Test`
- **Vercel config:** `vercel.json` sets `outputDirectory: "."`

## Architecture
- `index.html`, `learn.html`, `challenge.html`, `profile.html`, `leaderboard.html`, `settings.html` are separate pages.
- Every page loads `src/global.js`, `src/storage-proxy.js`, services, manifests, registry, components, and a page boot script.
- All shared code is attached to `window.CA`.
- `src/modules/manifests.js` defines `window.CA.CHALLENGE_MANIFESTS`.
- `src/modules/registry.js` provides challenge lookup helpers.
- `src/services/store.js` is a tiny reactive store.
- `src/services/progress.js` reads/writes challenge completion.
- `src/services/gamify.js` computes XP, levels, streaks, badges, and daily challenges.
- `src/storage-proxy.js` is the `db` service that persists to `localStorage` or `window.name`.
- `src/components/` contains self-registering Web Components.
- `src/labs/lab-runner.js` mounts labs into a Shadow DOM host on `file://` or a sandboxed `srcdoc` iframe on HTTP/HTTPS.
- `src/labs/sandbox-runtime.js` runs inside the iframe and dispatches `postMessage` events.
- `src/modules/<domain>/<challenge>/lab.js` registers a lab controller on `window.CA.labs`.
- `manifest.json` makes the app installable; no service worker is currently registered.

## Technologies
- HTML5: semantic elements, `<dialog>`, `<details>`, `<template>`, `<canvas>`, `<iframe sandbox>`.
- CSS: custom properties, `@layer`, mobile-first media queries (with container queries where appropriate), view transitions, `prefers-reduced-motion`. No inline styles; reusable external classes and CSS custom properties for dynamic values.
- JS: classic scripts, IIFEs, private fields, `async/await`, `structuredClone`.
- Storage: `localStorage` / `window.name`.

## Entry points
- Dashboard: `index.html`
- Learn: `learn.html`
- Challenge: `challenge.html?id=<challenge-id>`
- Other pages: `profile.html`, `leaderboard.html`, `settings.html`
- Manifest: `manifest.json`
- Vercel config: `vercel.json`

## Documentation index
- `AGENTS.md` — agent conventions, quick start, build/deploy, security rules, lab module API.
- `DESIGN.md` — design system (colors, typography, spacing, components, responsive, lab design).
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` — system design, module layout, data flow.
- `DATABASE.md` — storage schema and backends.
- `STYLEGUIDE.md` — naming, formatting, code conventions (mobile-first, no inline styles).
- `SECURITY.md` — threat model, sandbox, no-eval policy.
- `TESTING.md` — testing strategy, verification checklist.
- `PROMPTS.md` — reusable prompts and agent workflows.
- `MEMORY.md` — long-term context, recurring decisions, unresolved issues.
- `ROADMAP.md` — planned features, milestones, and potential new modules.
- `DECISIONS.md` — architecture decision records.
- `API.md` — client-only API.
- `CONTRIBUTING.md` — workflow for human contributors.
- `README.md` — human-facing quick start and project description.

## For agents
Before writing code, read `AGENTS.md`, `DESIGN.md`, and `STYLEGUIDE.md`. Add new labs under `src/modules/<domain>/<challenge>/lab.js` and register the challenge manifest in `src/modules/manifests.js`. Build lab UIs with the shared `.lab-*` classes in `src/styles/components.css` and avoid inline styles; dynamic values should use CSS custom properties. Keep all challenge code free of `eval` and user-supplied script execution.
